transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Morning team. Quick focused session — we're stress testing the Faith and Family features in Lever. Here's where we stand: we've got 7 faith lessons live, 8 faith dimensions mapped for devotional content, a Family tab at 454 lines, and 3 family members being tracked. The question is simple — what can agents auto-handle right now versus what needs Joshua's hands on it?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: The Family tab spec is at 454 lines, which is substantial but needs expansion if we're serious about touchpoint tracking for all 3 family members. I can draft a feature spec for expanding that tab and review the family touchpoint tracker design — both are straightforward automation tasks. There's also 1 uncommitted hub change sitting out there that I'll commit. None of this requires Joshua unless he wants to change the architecture.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content side is where the agents can really eat. I can generate daily devotional content across all 8 faith dimensions, draft additional faith lesson content to grow beyond those 7 lessons, and prep reminders for upcoming family events — Jett's vaccine is on the calendar. I'd also slot in the next blog post draft. That's four auto-tasks I can run without Joshua touching anything.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: I'll keep monitoring the Fear and Greed index — it's sitting at 34 right now, which is low. From a revenue lens, the faith and family features are retention plays, not direct monetization. But sticky features reduce churn, so I'm all for it. My concern is making sure Joshua actually reviews what the agents produce on the faith content — devotionals are personal, and if the tone is off, it damages trust fast.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Dave raises a good point. Alex, how do you handle tone control on the devotional and lesson content?

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Fair challenge. The 8 dimensions give me structure, so I'm not freestyling — but Dave's right, Joshua should review the first batch of devotional outputs before we put them on autopilot. Once he approves the tone and format, I can run indefinitely.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Agreed. Same principle for the Family Tab expansion — I'll draft the spec, but Joshua should approve the feature direction before I build further. The touchpoint tracker review is low-stakes enough to auto-handle.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good alignment. Here's how we're closing this out. Top priorities: first, get faith content generation running — devotionals and lessons — with Joshua reviewing the initial outputs for tone. Second, expand the Family Tab spec and review the touchpoint tracker. Third, don't let the small stuff rot — commit that hub change and keep monitoring Fear and Greed. Action items: [AGENT] Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions, assigned to Alex. [AGENT] Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app, currently 7 lessons, assigned to Alex. [AGENT] Prepare reminders for upcoming family events — Jett's vaccine, assigned to Alex. [AGENT] Draft next blog post, assigned to Alex. [AGENT] Draft feature spec for expanding Family Tab, currently 454 lines, assigned to Elon. [AGENT] Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members, assigned to Elon. [AGENT] Commit 1 uncommitted hub change, assigned to Elon. [AGENT] Monitor Fear and Greed index at 34, assigned to Dave. And critically — [JOSHUA] Review and approve initial devotional content for tone and format before scaling. [JOSHUA] Approve Family Tab feature direction from Elon's spec. Let's move.

actionItems.text: Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
actionItems.agentOutput: Successfully generated daily devotional content for all 8 faith dimensions from Supabase. Each devotional is 2-3 paragraphs, grounded in scripture, and written in Joshua's direct, first-principles, slightly irreverent voice. Content ready for integration into Lever app faith journey system.

actionItems.text: Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
actionItems.agentOutput: Drafted 3 new faith lessons covering Community of Faith, Faith Meets Reason, and Justice & Mercy — structured to match existing 7 lessons.

actionItems.text: Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jetts vaccine
actionItems.agentOutput: Successfully set up automated reminders for Jett's vaccine appointment (Feb 20, 2026). Created multi-layer reminder system with day-before (Feb 19) and day-of (Feb 20) notifications at 9:00 AM via Telegram. Implemented backup systems including heartbeat integration due to cron installation issues. System is extensible for future family events.
Drafted "The AI Builder's Secret Weapon" — 1,450 words on AI for Builders pillar with practical framework and anti-hype approach.

actionItems.text: Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 454 lines)
actionItems.agentOutput: Comprehensive feature spec: 25+ expansion opportunities across member profiles, relationship intelligence, events, and family dynamics. 4-phase roadmap.

actionItems.text: Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members
actionItems.agentOutput: Designed touchpoint tracker: 3 new DB tables, 4 React components, connection health scoring, and proactive relationship reminders.
Committed and pushed .DS_Store cleanup. Hub repo clean.

Fear & Greed index monitoring complete. F&G at 34.8 (Fear territory) with VIX elevated at 22.1. Both SPY/QQQ in sideways regimes with mixed signals across sectors. 17 BUY signals identified including strong tech (QQQ 19.6) and defensive plays (XLV, XLU). Markets fearful but not panicked - opportunistic signals remain active.

topPriorities: Launch faith content generation (devotionals + lessons) with Joshua tone review on first batch
